title: potentilla fruticosa subsp. floribunda
intro: Potentilla fruticosa subsp. floribunda, also known as flowering cinquefoil, is a shrubby perennial plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) native to mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its rich content of tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of flowering cinquefoil offer various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of Potentilla fruticosa subsp. floribunda include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
